name: ComfyUI
tagline: The connective tissue of open generative media
description: A node-graph GUI, API, and backend for running generative models locally. In 2026 it is the universal front end for open media: image (FLUX.2, Z-Image, Qwen-Image), video (Wan 2.2, LTX-2), audio, and 3D, composed into reusable visual workflows shareable as JSON.
why: Nearly every new open model ships with ComfyUI support within days, making it both a power tool and a de facto packaging format for generative pipelines. If open generative AI has a center of gravity, this is it.
useCases: Local image, video, and audio pipelines
An API backend for generative apps
audience: Power users, technical artists, studios, developers

setup.requirements: One-click desktop builds exist; NVIDIA, AMD ROCm, Intel Arc, or Apple Silicon; 6GB VRAM usable, 16-24GB comfortable

license.notes: GPL-3.0 matters if you embed it in a product; the models it runs each carry their own weights licenses.

limitations: Complex graphs have a real learning curve, and the custom-node ecosystem varies wildly in quality — treat third-party nodes with supply-chain caution.
take: Learn the graph once and every new model becomes a weekend, not a migration.
